methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aurelius Systems is a Seed-stage frontier-tech company operating in Defense & Security, having raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies (2 public, 8 private), including same-sector and adjacent-stage benchmarks such as Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x, implying a valuation of $25M. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables, with valuations ranging from $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A), yielding a median of $300M.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a valuation range of $21M (low) to $345M (high), with a median estimate of $162M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial uncertainty inherent to early-stage defense-tech valuations, driven primarily by the outsized positions of late-stage outliers like Scale AI within the peer set and the limited market comparability for pre-revenue frontier platforms. Stage progression suggests conservatism relative to peer medians; reconciliation requires clarity on Aurelius's competitive differentiation, IP maturity, and customer validation relative to named peers.
